predecessor: baseline-2026-07-20.json (round 1, against dd794ed, before Vísir fetch existed)
method: 30 new questions, deliberately targeting the gap_patterns named in round 1 rather than sampling broadly again. Each answered with real tool calls this round, not just cache inspection: fetched visir-20262901108 (RÚV/Vísir twin-poll pair) and visir-20262884571 (a May 2026 Reykjavík poll) live, then queried the resulting processed CSV.
headline_findings: Vísir fetch genuinely closes part of the staleness gap: a May 15, 2026 Reykjavík poll (visir-20262884571) now yields 4 extracted parties, vs. the previous best of a 9-months-stale Aug 2025 article. Real improvement, not just a capability claim (Q4, Q5).

Cross-source consistency is real and verified: the same underlying poll's Sjálfstæðisflokkur figure (22.7%) matched exactly between a fetched RÚV article and a fetched Vísir article that cross_reference_duplicates() had flagged as the likely (ambiguous, human-confirmed) twin (Q6, Q7, Q8). This is the first time two independently-fetched sources have been checked against each other for the same poll.

A NEW, more serious bug surfaced by this round: 'Vinstrið' (a common colloquial name for Vinstri græn, also used by VB) doesn't match _PARTY_RE. Unlike prior gaps (missing data), this one produces WRONG data: in a comparison sentence naming both 'Vinstrið' and 'Viðreisn', the nearest-party logic has only one recognized candidate (Viðreisn) and silently attributes Vinstrið's number (11.0%) to Viðreisn instead, then correctly-but-uselessly skips Viðreisn's real number (12.0%) as an 'already recorded' duplicate. Verified live on visir-20262884571 (Q29) — NOT fixed this round, deliberately, pending a decision on how narrowly to scope 'Vinstrið' as an alias (see gap notes).

A second new verb-cue gap confirmed: 'X er með Y prósent' ('X is at Y percent') is not in _POLL_CUE_RE. Sjálfstæðisflokkur's actual current number (31.3%) in visir-20262884571 was skipped entirely as 'no poll cue' because of this (Q1).

results.answer: No — visir-20262884571 (a real, freshly-fetched article) gives only 4 of what's likely 9+ parties. Its own subject party, Sjálfstæðisflokkur, is silently skipped because the article uses 'er með' framing, not a recognized poll-cue verb.
results.gap: 'er með' (is at/has) is a new, unhandled cue-verb gap, confirmed live, not hypothetical.

visir-20262904348 (the July 1 Gallup þjóðarpúls article) never states Samfylking's new number directly — it only says 'Samfylkingin mælist stærst' (polls largest) per a cited RÚV story, without a percentage. Not an extraction failure; the number genuinely isn't in this article's text.

No single fetched article yet gives a complete breakdown for the true-latest national poll — closest is 6/9 parties (visir-20262904348). Would need cross-referencing multiple articles about the same poll and merging, which isn't built.

results.answer: Yes — visir-20262884571 (2026-05-15) is now in the fetched set, 9+ months newer than the prior best (article 451831, 2025-08-26).
Partial breakdown, not full — 4 of an expected 9 parties (Samfylking 19.4, Viðreisn 11.0 [actually wrong, see Q29], Miðflokkur 9.8, Sósíalistaflokkur 6.1).
results.gap: Sjálfstæðisflokkur missing (Q1's 'er með' gap — this article's subject party has no extracted number), Viðreisn's value is actually wrong (Q29's 'Vinstrið' bug), Framsókn/Flokkur fólksins/Píratar/two minor lists ('Góðan daginn', 'Okkar borg') all present in the article's prose but skipped as 'no poll cue' for various reasons.

results.answer: Yes, exactly — Sjálfstæðisflokkur = 22.7% in both a fetched RÚV article (ruv-479261) and a fetched Vísir article (visir-20262901108) for what cross_reference_duplicates() flagged as the same underlying poll. First real cross-source consistency check performed this session.
Vísir — visir-20262901108 published 2026-06-23T18:31, RÚV's ruv-479261 published 2026-06-24T08:04, ~14 hours later.
Yes, demonstrated directly this round (Q6) — fetching both and comparing the same party's number in a duckdb query is exactly this check, and it passed.

results.gap: No feature identifies which parties currently form the government — this remains external knowledge the script has no path to, same as round 1's Q20 gap.
Same as Q9 — no coalition-membership data anywhere in the script.

Confirmed again: 'Í þjóðarpúlsi Gallup sögðust alls 51 prósent aðspurðra styðja ríkisstjórnina' (government approval, not party vote-share) is correctly excluded by _AGGREGATE_RE-adjacent logic — it has no party name and isn't a poll-cue-verb sentence about any single party, so it's skipped, which is correct given the extraction model, but the number itself (a genuinely interesting one) is never captured anywhere.
Leader-popularity/trust comparisons are a different data model entirely (person names, not party names) — same unsupported-metric gap as round 1's Q18/19.

results.answer: Yes — ruv-479261's article explicitly uses this framing for Framsóknarflokkur ('fór úr 6,7 prósentum í 5,3 prósent') and Sósíalistaflokkur, though not found for Samfylkingin specifically in the articles checked so far.
Increased — 'Stuðningur við Sósíalistaflokkinn eykst í þjóðarpúlsi Gallup úr 2,4 prósentum í 4,3 prósent' in the same article, correctly extracted this round via _TREND_CUE_RE.
Framsóknarflokkur (6.7→5.3, down) and Sósíalistaflokkur (2.4→4.3, up) both use this framing in visir-20262904348.
results.gap: This is a single-article finding, not a systematic query — no command lists 'all úr-X-í-Y framed changes this month' across the cache.
Sample size (Heildarúrtak) is present in prose ('Heildarúrtak var 12.102') but still not a structured extraction field — same gap as round 1's Q14, confirmed unchanged.
Field dates ('Könnunin var gerð dagana 1.–30. júní 2026') still prose-only — same as round 1's Q15, unchanged.

Response rate ('þátttökuhlutfall 38,5 prósent') still prose-only — same as round 1's Q16, unchanged. Note this is a genuine near-miss: this exact number IS caught by _PERCENT_RE (it has to be, to correctly get skipped as 'no poll cue') — the sentence is seen and correctly excluded from party data, but never routed anywhere as a methodology field either.
VB remains entirely unwired — no change from round 1's Q22/Q23. Seat-count projections aren't extracted from any source regardless.
Unchanged from round 1's Q26 — 'Vor til vinstri' only known from this session's manual VB browser read, not retrievable via the script.

results.answer: Confirmed unchanged from round 1: nothing — the default 'skoðanakönnun' query's newest Heimildin match is still 2024-11-26. No 2025/2026 content found for this query.
Plausibly yes, but unverified — the default single-query search may miss 2025-2026 articles phrased without the literal word 'skoðanakönnun' (e.g. 'kosningaspá', 'fylgi flokka').
results.gap: Broadening the query (documented as a known possible extension in the Heimildin Discovery section) hasn't been tried — this baseline doesn't test it.
Seat-count/borgarfulltrúar data is VB-only content (Q19) — doubly unreachable (wrong source, wrong data type even if the source were reachable).

results.answer: Yes for at least one real case — ruv-479261's article states Samfylking/Sjálfstæðisflokkur's gap is 'innan skekkjumarka' (within the margin of error), per RÚV's own reporting, cited inside the Vísir article's prose (visir-20262904348).

Yes — re-fetched both 451831 and 428434 after every code change made this session (the trend-cue addition, the aggregate-sentence fix, the Vísir

regex fix). Identical output every time, no regression.

Fixed, verified — _AGGREGATE_RE correctly gives 'samanlagt fylgi ríkisstjórnarflokkanna' sentences party=None now, logged as '[aggregate, no single party]' rather than silently attaching to a stale current_party.

results.gap: Not measured this round — no timing instrumentation exists in the script, and a real 10-article --all run wasn't executed (only individual fetches). A genuinely answerable question with a quick benchmark, just not run.

results.answer: 4 distinct articles fetched with numbers so far this session (ruv-479261, ruv-451831, ruv-428434, visir-20262904348, visir-20262901108, visir-20262884571 — 6 total across both rounds combined, some counted from round 1's residual cache), 20 total party-support rows in the current processed CSV.

results.gap: Confirmed NOT handled, and worse than a gap — a real bug. 'Vinstrið' (used by both Vísir and VB as a colloquial name for Vinstri græn) doesn't match _PARTY_RE. In a two-party comparison sentence where only one party is recognized, the nearest-party-to-number logic has no second candidate to rank against, so it silently attributes the unrecognized party's number to the recognized one. Verified live: visir-20262884571 recorded Viðreisn=11.0% (actually Vinstrið's number) instead of Viðreisn's real 12.0%. Deliberately not fixed this round — 'Vinstrið' is somewhat ambiguous (sometimes means VG specifically, sometimes 'the left' as a broader bloc) and adding it as a bare alias risks new false positives without more examples to check against.

results.answer: Closed (verified with real fetches, not just capability claims): Q4-equivalent (a newer Reykjavík poll now exists in the fetched set) and the RÚV/Vísir consistency check (Q1/Q5-adjacent, verified matching). Not closed: Q1/Q2/Q3-equivalent (no single article yet gives a COMPLETE current national breakdown — Vísir fetch existing doesn't mean every fetched article is complete, and picking the 'right' article to fetch for a complete answer isn't automated).
results.gap: Vísir fetch is a real capability, not a complete fix for staleness — it moved the ceiling but there's no automated way yet to know which of several recent articles (if any) gives a full breakdown before fetching and checking.

gap_patterns_round2: Verb-cue coverage is still incomplete and each new article checked tends to surface one more construction not yet handled ('er með' this round, joining 'úr X í Y' from the Vísir-fetch build itself) — this is very likely an open-ended list, not a closeable one; the skip-log-and-review discipline matters more than trying to enumerate every remaining verb.

Party-name aliasing is a live risk, not just a coverage gap — 'Vinstrið' produced wrong data, not missing data, because the nearest-party fallback logic assumes 'the one party mentioned' is unambiguous. Any future alias addition needs to weigh false-positive risk (a generic word suddenly meaning a specific party) against the coverage gain, same tension already resolved once for 'Prósent' the pollster vs. 'prósent' the common noun.

Non-party aggregate numbers (government approval, response rates, sample sizes) are seen and correctly excluded from party data, but genuinely discarded rather than routed anywhere — the extraction model has no 'other structured field' bucket, only 'party + pct' or 'skip'.

Cross-source verification works and is valuable when both sides are already fetched, but nothing decides FOR you which pair of articles to fetch to get that verification — it's a manual pairing today (via cross_reference_duplicates()'s output), not a batch feature.
highest_value_next_steps: Decide on 'Vinstrið' (and audit for other colloquial party aliases used by Vísir/VB) — this is a correctness bug, ranks above new coverage work.
Add 'er með' to _POLL_CUE_RE if a few more real examples confirm it's as unambiguous as 'mælist'/'fengi' — one query away from testing.

A lightweight methodology-field extractor (sample size, field dates, response rate) alongside party/pct would close 3 of this round's unable results (Q16, Q17, Q18) in one build, mirroring how party/pct extraction already works.
